cards.def: Up to date with the latest news, ideas, or information; side by side.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Keep abreast of recent market developments"
cards.context: Appears in sentences where a researcher or professional must stay informed about rapidly changing data.
cards.mnem: A breast — Imagine walking side-by-side, chest-to-chest, keeping pace perfectly with someone.
cards.vis: Two runners dashing down a track perfectly side by side, reading newspapers at the exact same time to stay updated.

cards.def: To cause surprise or confusion in someone, especially by acting against their expectations.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Confound the critics with a brilliant performance"
cards.context: Used when an unexpected scientific finding completely baffles experts or contradicts established theories.
cards.mnem: Con-found — You 'found' a 'con' artist's trick, which completely confuses and surprises you.
cards.vis: A magician pulls a live elephant out of a tiny top hat, leaving the entire audience scratching their heads in absolute bewilderment.

cards.def: A temporary departure from the main subject in speech or writing.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Apologize for a lengthy digression during the lecture"
cards.context: Appears in prompts evaluating the structure of an essay or argument that wanders off-topic.
cards.mnem: Di-gression — 'Di' (two/away) + 'gress' (walk). Walking away into a side path.
cards.vis: A train suddenly veers off its main iron tracks onto a dirt path just to chase a shiny butterfly in a field.

cards.def: A lack of compatibility or similarity between two or more facts.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A major discrepancy in the financial accounts"
cards.context: Signals a conflict or contradiction between empirical evidence and theoretical predictions.
cards.mnem: Dis-scrap-ancy — A 'scrap' or fight between two numbers that don't match up.
cards.vis: An accountant looking at two balance sheets where one says $10 and the other says $10 million, causing a giant error siren to blare.

cards.def: Deceitful; double-dealing in behavior or speech.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Expose the duplicitous tactics of the politician"
cards.context: Used to describe characters, politicians, or business entities that feign cooperation while plotting betrayal.
cards.mnem: Duplicity / Duplicate — Having a duplicate, secret second face to deceive people.
cards.vis: A two-faced villain literally unzipping their friendly human mask to reveal a grinning devil underneath.

cards.def: Convenient and practical, although possibly improper or immoral.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Choose an expedient solution to bypass the bureaucracy"
cards.context: Contrasted against principled actions; denotes doing what works quickly rather than what is morally right.
cards.mnem: Expedite — To speed things up, an expedient solution is the fastest shortcut available.
cards.vis: A corrupt politician cutting a giant red tape ribbon with golden scissors to pass a law instantly under the table.

cards.def: Invent or concoct something, typically with deceitful intent; construct or manufacture.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Fabricate an alibi to avoid getting caught"
cards.context: In context shifts, it denotes inventing data or stories out of thin air to cover up a failure.
cards.mnem: Fabric-ate — Knitting a complex blanket of lies from raw fabric.
cards.vis: A dishonest scientist in a lab coats sewing together fake dinosaur bones out of papier-mâché to fool a museum.

cards.def: Looking or feeling dejected; morose and downcast.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Sitting alone with a glum expression on his face"
cards.context: Used to describe the somber or pessimistic mood of a person or group facing bad news.
cards.mnem: Gloom / Glue — Feeling so sad and heavy that you are glued to your chair in misery.
cards.vis: A sad rainy cloud floating indoors, soaking a depressed person sitting on a couch weeping over dropped ice cream.

cards.def: A person or thing that announces or signals the approach of another; a forerunner.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A sudden drop in stock prices was a harbinger of economic crisis"
cards.context: Acts as a clue for cause-and-effect or chronological predictions in text completion.
cards.mnem: Bring-er — A herald who 'brings' the news of what is coming ahead.
cards.vis: A dark, cloaked figure blowing a horn as storm clouds instantly gather directly behind him.

cards.def: Belonging naturally; essential, inherent, or constitutional.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "The intrinsic value of a good education"
cards.context: Contrasted with extrinsic (external) factors; defines qualities that are fundamentally part of an object's nature.
cards.mnem: In-twin-sic — Inwardly built into the very soul or center of the object.
cards.vis: A glowing core inside a rough, plain-looking rock that represents its true, unchangeable internal value.

cards.def: Generosity in bestowing money or gifts upon others.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Depend on the largesse of wealthy philanthropists"
cards.context: Used when describing patrons funding the arts or providing massive endowments to universities.
cards.mnem: Large-ness — Having a large heart and giving out large piles of cash.
cards.vis: A wealthy individual standing on a balcony, throwing gold coins out of a giant sack down to a cheering crowd.

cards.def: A person, especially a man, who behaves without moral principles or a sense of responsibility, especially in sexual matters.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "The notorious libertine spent his fortune on wild parties"
cards.context: Describes historical or literary figures known for rejecting societal constraints in favor of hedonism.
cards.mnem: Liberty-gone — Taking personal 'liberty' to an extreme, reckless, immoral level.
cards.vis: A chaotic partygoer swinging from a chandelier while drinking out of two bottles at once, laughing at rules.

cards.def: Wrongdoing, especially by a public official.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Corporate executives investigated for financial malfeasance"
cards.context: Typically triggers negative context clues involving corruption, audits, or legal prosecution of authorities.
cards.mnem: Mal-fees-ance — 'Mal' means bad. Taking 'bad fees' or bribes as a crooked official.
cards.vis: A mayor caught on camera shoving stacks of bribe money into a safe hidden behind a judicial portrait.

cards.def: Clear or obvious to the eye or mind; to display or show a quality or feeling by one's acts or appearance.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Her deep anxiety manifested as a nervous tic"
cards.context: Used both as an adjective (apparent, overt) and a verb (to demonstrate, reveal).
cards.mnem: Man-in-vest — A man wearing a bright neon vest is completely obvious and visible to everyone.
cards.vis: A ghost turning from invisible to solid, bright form right in the middle of a crowded room for all to see.

cards.def: Extremely small; so small as to be precise or insignificant.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Examine the specimen for minute changes in structure"
cards.context: Refers to microscopic details, subtle differences, or highly meticulous analysis (e.g., minute attention to detail).
cards.mnem: Minute (time) — A single minute is a tiny, small fraction of a whole day.
cards.vis: A detective looking through a giant magnifying glass to examine an incredibly tiny speck of dust on a counter.

cards.def: Conforming to or following what is currently popular and fashionable.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Decorate the loft with modish contemporary art"
cards.context: Often used with a slightly critical tone in GRE passages to indicate fleeting, superficial trends over timeless value.
cards.mnem: Mode-ish — In the current fashion 'mode' or trendy style.
cards.vis: A model strutting down a runway wearing an absurdly avant-garde outfit made entirely of glowing neon plastic.

cards.def: Just coming into existence and beginning to display signs of future potential.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Invest early in the nascent aerospace industry"
cards.context: Identifies early stages of a movement, technology, or idea before it becomes fully mature.
cards.mnem: New-sent — Something freshly created and 'sent' out into the world.
cards.vis: A tiny green seedling bursting aggressively through hard concrete, glowing with potential energy.

cards.def: Lasting or existing for a long or apparently infinite time; enduring or continually recurring.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Inflation remains a perennial problem for developing nations"
cards.context: Indicates persistent problems or timeless issues that reappear year after year.
cards.mnem: Per-annual — Lasting 'throughout the years' without ever dying out.
cards.vis: A beautiful flower that instantly resprouts and blooms out of the snow every single winter without fail.

cards.def: Devoutly religious; making a hypocritical display of virtue.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Offer pious prayers at the altar; tired of her pious platitudes"
cards.context: Can be positive (sincere devotion) or negative/pejorative (sanctimonious, holier-than-thou attitude).
cards.mnem: Pie-us — A hypocritically holy person saying 'God wants a slice of pie for us, but mostly for me'.
cards.vis: A priest praying intensely with eyes rolled up, while covertly stealing coins from the donation basket.

cards.def: Occurring at a favorable time; opportune; involving divine foresight or intervention.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A providential breeze saved the ship from crashing into the rocks"
cards.context: Signals a lucky, timely occurrence that saves a situation from disaster.
cards.mnem: Provide-ential — The universe 'provides' exactly what you need at the perfect moment.
cards.vis: An umbrella suddenly falling out of the sky right into the hands of a man the exact second a downpour starts.

cards.def: Skill or expertise in a particular activity or field; bravery in battle.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Renowned for her extraordinary athletic prowess"
cards.context: Used to praise exceptional capability, scientific talent, or military competence.
cards.mnem: Proud-prowess — Being highly proud of your incredible, unmatched skills.
cards.vis: A warrior effortlessly catching arrows mid-air with one hand while solving a Rubik's cube with the other.

cards.def: A split or division between strongly opposed sections or parties, caused by differences in opinion or belief.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A deep schism within the political party over leadership"
cards.context: Signals polarization, factionalism, or ideological breakups within groups.
cards.mnem: Scissors — Action like a pair of scissors cutting a unified organization right down the middle.
cards.vis: A massive, jagged crack opening up in the middle of a boardroom table, splitting the board members into two yelling groups.

cards.def: The action or crime of making a false spoken statement damaging to a person's reputation.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Accuse the rival company of spreading malicious slander"
cards.context: Appears in contexts involving character assassination, defamation, or political attacks.
cards.mnem: Slang-der — Using mean, fabricated 'slang' words to drag someone's name through the mud.
cards.vis: A snake with a microphone whispering false, venomous rumors about a town hero into a megaphone.

cards.def: Loyal, reliable, and hardworking; strongly built and sturdy.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A stalwart supporter of the environmental movement"
cards.context: Used to praise an unshakeable ally or a firm defender of a particular policy or doctrine.
cards.mnem: Tall-wall — Like a tall, brick wall that cannot be knocked down or broken.
cards.vis: A massive knight in shining armor standing unmoved in a raging storm, holding up a heavy shield to protect a castle door.

cards.def: Ask or beg for something earnestly or humbly.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Supplicate the king for royal clemency"
cards.context: Indicates submission or desperate pleading in the face of an absolute authority.
cards.mnem: Supply-cate — Begging a powerful entity to please 'supply' you with mercy or food.
cards.vis: A peasant down on both knees, hands clasped tightly, pleading before a giant golden throne.

cards.def: Sparing in the use of words; abrupt or concise to the point of rudeness.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Issue a terse, one-word denial to the press"
cards.context: Often contrasted against verbose or prolix language; can imply irritation or efficient brevity.
cards.mnem: Tense / Terse — When you are tense, you give short, blunt, curt answers.
cards.vis: A grumpy boss slamming down a paper that simply has a giant red 'NO' written on it instead of an explanation.

cards.def: A long, angry speech of criticism or accusation.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Launch into a furious tirade against the new regulations"
cards.context: Signals a radical shift to hostile, unmeasured criticism or emotional outbursts.
cards.mnem: Tired — You are totally 'tired' of listening to someone's long, angry rant.
cards.vis: A coach at halftime screaming so hard that fire literally flies out of his mouth, turning his players to ash.

cards.def: Applicable everywhere or to all cases; affecting or done by all people or things in the world.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A universal human right to freedom of expression"
cards.context: Contrasted with particular, regional, or localized phenomena.
cards.mnem: Universe — Covering the entire universe; leaving absolutely nothing out.
cards.vis: A giant blanket extending across the entire globe, wrapping every single country up simultaneously.

cards.def: Defeat thoroughly or completely in a battle or competition.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Vanquish the opposing army in a decisive battle"
cards.context: Used metaphorically or literally to describe total elimination of an opponent, disease, or obstacle.
cards.mnem: Vanish — To defeat your enemies so completely that they utterly 'vanish' from the earth.
cards.vis: A champion boxer landing a punch so powerful that his opponent turns into a cloud of smoke and disappears.

cards.def: Characterized by, expressive of, or causing sorrow or misery; wretchedly bad or deplorable.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "The team's performance was woeful, resulting in a 6-0 loss"
cards.context: Can describe sad emotional states or highlight an abysmal, inadequate standard of work (e.g., woeful lack of preparation).
cards.mnem: Woe is me — Crying out 'woe!' because a situation is utterly terrible and pathetic.
cards.vis: A student looking at a report card filled entirely with 'F' grades while weeping into a giant puddle of tears.
